primary_goal: improve Codeberg-first routing on an already-live third-party listing that can send qualified evaluator traffic
why_this_action: The freshest audit says Codeberg adoption is flat and same-family directory/curator bursts should pause.
A live listing correction is higher leverage than another net-new submission because traffic can already land here now.
ToolWise already routes to Codeberg correctly, while SaaSHub still routes the hero CTA to the website and exposes a wrong GitHub repo button.

evidence.backlink_refresh_result: 2 live listings confirmed: SaaSHub and ToolWise
evidence.toolwise_repo_routing: Codeberg primary repo detected in listing HTML
evidence.saashub_repo_routing: Page HTML contains hero CTA to https://ralphworkflow.com and GitHub button to https://github.com/mistlight/Ralph-Workflow; no Codeberg primary repo link detected in raw HTML

actions_taken.note: Comment requires SaaSHub moderation before becoming visible.

expected_outcome: SaaSHub updates the live listing to point evaluators at Codeberg first and the correct GitHub mirror second, reducing misrouted repo traffic.
replacement_condition: If the SaaSHub listing still routes incorrectly or no follow-up signal appears by 2026-05-31, retire listing-correction follow-up for this target and switch the next fresh action to a different high-intent demand-capture lane.
